project: Recipe Club Hub - Meals Simplification & Enhancements
description: Simplify the Meals tab by removing unused AI features, remove recipe sharing, fix recipe filtering, add grocery list to Meals tab, add meal completion with rating, and add recipe photo/PDF upload with AI parsing.

userStories.title: Remove AI features from Meals tab
userStories.description: As a user, I want a simplified Meals tab without AI suggestions, chat, or preferences so that the interface is clean and focused on meal planning.
acceptanceCriteria: PreferencesDialog.tsx, AISuggestionPanel.tsx, AIChatPanel.tsx, and SuggestionCard.tsx are deleted
generate-meal-suggestions edge function directory is deleted
MealPlanPage.tsx no longer imports or references AI components, preferences state, suggestions state, chatMessages state, or isLoadingSuggestions state
MealPlanPage.tsx no longer calls loadPreferences(), handleGetSuggestions(), or handleChatMessage()
The Meals tab UI shows only the week navigation, meal grid, and Add Meal dialog (no AI panels, no preferences button, no Get Suggestions button)
All test files for deleted components are removed
MealPlanPage.test.tsx is updated to remove all AI-related test cases
Tests for remaining components still pass with 100% coverage on mealplan directory
Typecheck passes (npm run build)
Tests pass (npm run test:coverage)
userStories.notes: Files to delete: src/components/mealplan/PreferencesDialog.tsx (275 lines), src/components/mealplan/AISuggestionPanel.tsx (74 lines), src/components/mealplan/AIChatPanel.tsx (89 lines), src/components/mealplan/SuggestionCard.tsx (55 lines), supabase/functions/generate-meal-suggestions/index.ts. Also delete their test files in tests/unit/. MealPlanPage.tsx (529 lines) has AI state on lines ~39-46, loadPreferences() function, handleGetSuggestions(), handleChatMessage(). Remove the AI panel rendering and preferences button from the JSX. The MealPlanPage.test.tsx is ~62KB and will need significant AI test removal. Keep WeekNavigation, MealPlanGrid, MealPlanSlot, AddMealDialog intact.

userStories.title: Remove recipe sharing and saving features
userStories.description: As a user, I want the recipe interface without sharing/saving clutter so that recipes are organized simply into Club and Personal tabs.
acceptanceCriteria: SharedWithMeSection.tsx, ShareRecipeDialog.tsx, and SaveRecipeButton.tsx are deleted
send-recipe-share edge function directory is deleted
RecipeHub.tsx no longer has 'shared' sub-tab, savedRecipeIds state, loadSavedRecipeIds(), handleSaveToggle(), or saved_recipes queries
RecipeHub.tsx RecipeSubTab type is 'club' | 'personal' only
RecipeHub.tsx no longer accepts or uses share_only accessType
RecipeCard.tsx no longer has Share button, ShareRecipeDialog, SaveRecipeButton, or isSaved prop
Dashboard.tsx no longer has isShareOnly or share_only logic
auth.ts no longer has share_only in access_type union
All test files for deleted components are removed
RecipeHub.test.tsx and RecipeCard.test.tsx are updated to remove sharing/saving tests
Typecheck passes (npm run build)
Tests pass with 100% coverage on recipes directory (npm run test:coverage)
userStories.notes: Delete: src/components/recipes/SharedWithMeSection.tsx (191 lines), src/components/recipes/ShareRecipeDialog.tsx (174 lines), src/components/recipes/SaveRecipeButton.tsx (63 lines), supabase/functions/send-recipe-share/index.ts (156 lines). Also delete tests: SharedWithMeSection.test.tsx, ShareRecipeDialog.test.tsx, SaveRecipeButton.test.tsx, send-recipe-share.test.ts. In RecipeHub.tsx: remove SharedWithMeSection import (line 18), savedRecipeIds state (line 45), loadSavedRecipeIds (lines 48-61), handleSaveToggle (lines 391-401), 'shared' button (lines 451-460), SharedWithMeSection render (lines 463-466), isSaved prop on RecipeCard (line 537). In RecipeCard.tsx: remove Share2 import (line 6), SaveRecipeButton import (line 9), ShareRecipeDialog import (line 10), isSaved prop (lines 41,47), showShareDialog state (line 53), share button (lines 100-108), SaveRecipeButton (lines 111-117), ShareRecipeDialog render (lines 273-281). In Dashboard.tsx: remove isShareOnly (line 46) and accessType prop (line 341). In auth.ts: change access_type to just 'club' (line 10, 34).

userStories.title: Fix recipe filtering - personal vs club
userStories.description: As a user, I want My Recipes to show only my personal recipes and Club Recipes to show only club recipes so that recipes are properly categorized.
acceptanceCriteria: loadPersonalRecipes() in RecipeHub.tsx only queries recipes where created_by=userId AND event_id IS NULL
loadPersonalRecipes() does NOT query saved_recipes table
loadPersonalRecipes() does NOT build savedRecipes array or merge it with personalRecipes
setRecipes() in loadPersonalRecipes is called with just personalRecipes (no saved recipes)
loadClubRecipes() still queries recipes where event_id IS NOT NULL (unchanged)
RecipeHub.test.tsx tests updated: remove saved recipe dedup tests, update personal recipe loading tests
Typecheck passes (npm run build)
Tests pass with 100% coverage (npm run test:coverage)
userStories.notes: RecipeHub.tsx loadPersonalRecipes() (lines 171-305) currently loads both personal recipes AND saved_recipes in a Promise.all. After US-002 removes savedRecipeIds, this function should be simplified to: (1) query only recipes with created_by=userId AND event_id IS NULL (the .is('event_id', null) was already added), (2) remove the saved_recipes query entirely, (3) remove savedRecipes building (lines 232-270), (4) remove dedup logic (lines 232-233), (5) just call setRecipes(personalRecipes). Also remove isSaved: false from the recipe mapping since the field won't exist anymore. The RecipeWithNotes interface in RecipeHub.tsx (line 21-28) should drop isSaved.

userStories.title: Apply pantry filtering to per-recipe ingredient views
userStories.description: As a user, I want pantry items filtered from individual recipe ingredient lists too (not just the combined list) so that my grocery views are consistent.
acceptanceCriteria: GroceryListSection.tsx applies filterPantryItems() to per-recipe tab ingredient lists
When viewing a single recipe's tab in the grocery section, pantry items are excluded
The combined/smart grocery view continues to filter pantry items as before
This behavior works in both Club Events (EventDetailPage) and will work for Meals (future US-005)
Tests updated for GroceryListSection to verify pantry filtering on per-recipe tabs
Typecheck passes (npm run build)
Tests pass with 100% coverage (npm run test:coverage)
userStories.notes: In GroceryListSection.tsx (258 lines), the per-recipe tabs (around lines 200-240) display raw recipe ingredients without pantry filtering. The combined view (lines 55-60) already applies filterPantryItems(). Fix: in the per-recipe tab rendering, filter each recipe's ingredients through filterPantryItems() before display. The filterPantryItems() function is imported from src/lib/groceryList.ts. The pantryItems prop is already passed to GroceryListSection. EventDetailPage.tsx already passes pantryItems correctly.

userStories.title: Add Groceries tab to Meals page
userStories.description: As a user, I want a Groceries tab in the Meals page that shows a combined weekly grocery list and per-recipe breakdowns so I can shop for my meal plan.
acceptanceCriteria: MealPlanPage has a tab switcher with 'Meal Plan' and 'Groceries' options
Groceries tab loads recipe_ingredients for all meals in the current week
Groceries tab loads user's pantry items via getPantryItems()
Groceries tab renders GroceryListSection with combined + per-recipe views
Pantry items are filtered from both combined and per-recipe views
Switching weeks reloads grocery data for the new week
Empty state shown when no meals have parsed ingredients
Tests added for Groceries tab loading and display
Typecheck passes (npm run build)
Tests pass with 100% coverage on mealplan directory (npm run test:coverage)
userStories.notes: MealPlanPage.tsx needs a new view state ('grid' | 'groceries'). Reuse existing components: GroceryListSection from src/components/recipes/GroceryListSection.tsx, combineIngredients/groupByCategory/filterPantryItems from src/lib/groceryList.ts, getPantryItems from src/lib/pantry.ts. To load ingredients: query meal_plan_items for current week's plan to get recipe_ids, then query recipe_ingredients for those recipe_ids. Also need to build a recipeNameMap (recipe_id -> recipe name) and recipeContentMap for GroceryListSection. The tab switcher should sit alongside WeekNavigation. WeekNavigation should remain visible in both tabs.

userStories.title: Add meal completion with optional rating
userStories.description: As a user, I want to mark meals as cooked and optionally rate recipes so I can track what I've made and remember what was good.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration adds cooked_at TIMESTAMPTZ column to meal_plan_items table
MealPlanSlot shows visual indicator for cooked meals (e.g., checkmark, different styling)
MealPlanSlot has a 'Mark as Cooked' action button
Clicking 'Mark as Cooked' opens EventRatingDialog in 'rating' mode with the slot's recipes
After submitting ratings, meal_plan_items.cooked_at is set to current timestamp
Cooked meals can be 'uncooked' (reset cooked_at to null)
MealPlanPage handles the rating dialog state and cooked status updates
Tests added for cooked state display, mark as cooked flow, and rating integration
Typecheck passes (npm run build)
Tests pass with 100% coverage (npm run test:coverage)
userStories.notes: Reuse EventRatingDialog from src/components/events/EventRatingDialog.tsx (275 lines). It has two modes: 'completing' (all recipes required) and 'rating' (at least one). Use 'rating' mode for meals. The dialog needs recipes as props - build from meal_plan_items that have recipe_ids. Migration should be in supabase/migrations/ with timestamp naming. MealPlanSlot.tsx (103 lines) renders individual cells - add cooked visual state. MealPlanPage.tsx handles all state - add ratingDialogOpen, selectedSlotForRating states. When rating is submitted, update meal_plan_items SET cooked_at = now() for items in that slot.

userStories.title: Add recipe photo/PDF upload with AI parsing
userStories.description: As a user, I want to upload a photo or PDF of a recipe and have it automatically parsed for ingredients and instructions so I can digitize physical recipes.
acceptanceCriteria: EventDetailPage add-recipe form has a prominent photo/PDF upload option alongside URL input
AddMealDialog Custom tab has a photo/PDF upload option
Uploaded files go to Supabase 'recipe-images' storage bucket
After upload, the resulting storage URL is used as the recipe URL and parse-recipe is triggered
Parse-recipe edge function correctly handles image files (jpg, png, heic, webp) and PDFs via Claude vision API
Parsed ingredients and instructions are saved to recipe_ingredients and recipe_content tables
Upload shows progress/loading state during parse
Error state shown if parsing fails
Tests added for upload-to-parse flow in both EventDetailPage and AddMealDialog
Typecheck passes (npm run build)
Tests pass with 100% coverage (npm run test:coverage)
userStories.notes: Existing infrastructure: PhotoUpload component (src/components/recipes/PhotoUpload.tsx, 192 lines) handles image/PDF upload to Supabase storage. parse-recipe edge function (supabase/functions/parse-recipe/index.ts, 393 lines) already detects Supabase storage URLs (line 105), downloads as base64 (lines 108-121), and sends to Claude vision API. The 'recipe-images' bucket is already configured. EventDetailPage already has a file upload flow (lines 588-632) that uploads to recipe-images bucket. For AddMealDialog.tsx (297 lines), add a similar upload flow in the Custom tab. Key flow: user uploads image -> file goes to Supabase storage -> get public URL -> create recipe record with that URL -> invoke parse-recipe edge function -> show parsed results.

userStories.title: Final dead code cleanup and coverage verification
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all dead code removed and 100% test coverage verified so the codebase is clean and maintainable.
acceptanceCriteria: No unused imports, variables, or functions remain in modified files
No orphaned type definitions from removed features
npm run build passes with no TypeScript errors
npm run test:coverage shows 100% on all required directories (events, ingredients, mealplan, recipes, lib)
npm run lint passes with no errors
IngredientWheel.tsx remains the only exception (~55% coverage acceptable)
userStories.notes: This is the final cleanup story. Run npm run test:coverage and check every file in the coverage report. Fix any gaps. Common issues: removed components still imported somewhere, unused type exports, test files importing deleted mocks. Run npm run build to catch any TypeScript errors. Run npm run lint to catch style issues. The coverage report 'Uncovered Line #s' column shows exactly what needs tests.

userStories.title: Evaluate all user flows and identify areas of improvement
userStories.description: As a product owner, I want a thorough evaluation of every user flow in the app so that I can identify UX gaps, broken flows, inconsistencies, and opportunities for improvement.
acceptanceCriteria: Document every user flow in the app: creating/managing club events, adding/viewing/rating recipes, managing ingredients and the wheel, meal planning (add/edit/remove/complete meals), grocery lists (club events and meals), recipe notes and photos, user authentication and onboarding

For each flow, identify: steps that feel clunky or require too many clicks, missing error states or empty states, inconsistencies between similar flows (e.g., club events vs meals), dead ends where the user gets stuck, missing navigation or back buttons
Evaluate grocery list consistency: does the combined list work the same way in Events and Meals? Are pantry filters applied consistently? Is the per-recipe view useful?

Evaluate recipe management: is it clear what's a personal vs club recipe? Can users easily find, filter, and navigate between recipes? Do recipe details (notes, ratings, ingredients, instructions) display well?
Evaluate meal plan UX: is the weekly grid intuitive? Is adding meals easy? Is the cooked/rating flow clear? Does the grocery tab integrate smoothly?
Evaluate event completion flow: is the rating dialog clear? Do members know they can rate after event completion? Is the transition from scheduled to completed smooth?
Write findings to ralph/ux-audit.md with sections for each flow area, severity ratings (critical/high/medium/low), and specific actionable recommendations
Create a prioritized list of top 10 improvements that would have the highest user impact
Typecheck passes (npm run build)
Tests pass (npm run test:coverage)
userStories.notes: This is an audit/analysis story, not a code change story. Read through every page and component in the app systematically. Key pages to evaluate: src/pages/Dashboard.tsx (main hub with tabs), src/pages/EventDetailPage.tsx (club event detail ~1800 lines), src/pages/PersonalMealDetailPage.tsx (meal detail ~917 lines), src/pages/Index.tsx (landing). Key component directories: src/components/events/ (club events), src/components/recipes/ (recipe hub, cards, grocery), src/components/mealplan/ (meal planning), src/components/ingredients/ (ingredient management), src/components/wheel/ (ingredient wheel). Look at navigation patterns in src/App.tsx. Check for consistent patterns: how errors are shown (toast vs inline), how loading states work, how empty states look, how dialogs behave. Compare the club event recipe flow vs the personal meal recipe flow for inconsistencies. Output goes to ralph/ux-audit.md - this file will inform future development priorities.
